Cr usader XP Value: 6,000 GP Value: 30,000 The Mines of Bloodstom: Crusader, a long sword +5, holy aveng er, pro­ vides 50% MR in a 5-foot radius, dispel magic at 17th levd in a 5-foot radius, + 10 damage vs. chaotic evil opponents. If a tanar'n is slain with Crusader, the sword gains following special pow­ ers: heal once a day, strength once per day when lighting evil opponents, charm person on contact once per day . The swo rd will disinte gra te a lanar'ri on a natural (unmodified) 20. It is tele­ pathiC with its owner, and can commune with the minor deity Lali bela onc
[PDF_PAGE 203 | score=10] - Sih'cr As with the gold dragon tooth, a silver dragon's tooth also requires one ingre­ dient from the white dragon list in order to achieve the dual immunity to cold and paralysis. Venom from a carrion crawler , giant Portuguese man-a -war, giant wasp, crystal ooze, drider, lene­ braus worm, assassin bug, fire snake, garhug, grell (a large amount), lenta­ mon, or female tiger fly is also needed. White Ice load blood, hoar fox blood, ice lizard blood, or a frost man's eye (the one covered by the patch). A fier an appropriate ingred ient is found and put into the basin, a low fire is kept burning unde r the basi n, and the tooth is allowed to steep for one hour. The wizard must then cast a l
[PDF_PAGE 192 | score=9] , thieves, and some include boosts to slats. Spell books contain spells and surprises. Most are restricted to priests and wizards, but a few may be used by any class. Tomes may be used by all classes and some grant boosts to slats. All magical books, librams, manuals, and lomes appear to be normal works of arcane lore. Each is indistinguishable from others by visual examina­ tion or by magical detection. A wish ~pcl1 can identify or class ify a magical work. Other spe lls, notably alter reality, com­ mune, contact higher planes, limited wish, and troe seeing are useless. A wish reveals the general contents of a book, telling what classes or charac­ teristics are most affected (nol necessaril

[PDF_PAGE 158 | score=5] Stalker Xt'Vallle: 2,000 GPVa llle: 10,000 Howl From the North Sialker is an enigmatic and powerful weapon that reveals its full powers on ly in the hands of a ranger. To anyone else, it is merely a long sword + /. It has a slcnder, long blue steel blade, but its hilt, pommel, and qui lions are made of a stark black meta l that is as hard as adamantite. If Stalker is stared at for any length of time, it seems to have wisps of shadowstuff moving silently across its surface, although this is just illusory ... or is it? Slulker is a +3 neutral good weapon when used by a ranger. The ranger gains a + I bonus to Wis­ dom and Intelligence while in possession of this blade. The ranger also gains a +
[PDF_PAGE 212 | score=5] , a nasty barb at its lip and a b lood groove that runs the length of the blade. The hilt of the dag­ ger is wrapped in monIed snake skin, though none can say for certai n what type of ser pent it came from. The pomme l bears a bright (some would say flaming) red garnet. The Triad of Betrayal was created for the god Hiddukel by Ihe mastersmith-god Reorx. Then , Hiddukel imbued each of the artifacts with his essence and set them free in the world, to thwart the will of the goddess Mishakal . The Triad of Betrayal was Hiddukel 's answer to Mishakal's Circle of Love, and each of the evil artifacts fol­ lows its good counterpart. The Triad first appeared in the Age of Might, when Hiddukel convin
[PDF_PAGE 302 | score=5] , of Valor XPVa]uc; 5,000 GP Value: 25,000 Tome of Magic This golden horn is indi slinguishable from any other magica l born until it is sounded. When souDdcd, each unit hearing it, that is allied with or loyal to the charac ter sounding the hom gains the following benefits. First , the unit gains a +2 bonus to its morale for 1 d4 BATTlESYSTEM rules turns. Second, any routed friendly unit who hears it immediately makes a rally check with a bonus of2 to its morale (for that check only) . The check is made when the horn is sounded-du ring the magic phase- rather than in the rally phase. If this additio nal check fails, the unit is entitled to a second check in th e rally phase, as normal. Enem
